identity.name: Cross-Plane Correlation MOAT — EDR + NDR + ITDR Stitch Into One Incident (T1078 / T1071.001 / T1110.003)
identity.summary: Three coordinated signals from one src_host within a ±30s window — an outbound HTTP beacon (NDR), an interactive bash spawned under the www-data service identity (EDR), and a Kerberos pre-auth failure burst against seeded AD service accounts (ITDR) — must stitch into a single XSIAM incident whose causality chain spans all three planes. The flagship Cortex MOAT scenario: detection lives less in…
identity.description: Real enterprise breaches rarely surface on a single telemetry plane: an operator beacons out (network), pivots a service-account shell on the host (endpoint), and probes Active Directory or the IdP (identity) — each leg looking benign in isolation. The competitive moat for XSIAM is cross-plane causality: stitching those independent signals from the same source into ONE incident so the SOC sees a coherent story instead of three low-severity alerts in three consoles. This card encodes the three per-plane anchor detections — an NDR beacon to a known IOC domain on panw_ngfw_traffic_raw, an EDR interactive-bash-from-www-data BIOC on xdr_data ENUM.PROCESS, and an ITDR Kerberos pre-auth failure burst on ad_audit / okta_sso / msft_azure_ad_signin — and then the correlation rule that fuses them. The stitch validation query groups by the source host and asserts count_distinct(_product) across planes resolves to a single incident_id with a causality chain of length >= 5 inside the ±30s correlation window. Detection success is measured as the Cross-Source Correlation Rate: over a batch of runs, the proportion that produce one stitched incident spanning {EDR, NDR, ITDR}.

techniques.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: Unix Shell

data_sources: Active Directory: Active Directory Credential Request

execution.framework_reference_id: TC-IR-05 — CortexSim cross-plane stitch (NDR beacon + EDR service-account shell + ITDR Kerberos pre-auth burst from one src_host)

prerequisites.description: Lab environment matching scenario SIM-MP-005 requirements: EDR, NDR and ITDR modules all reporting into the same XSIAM tenant from the same src_host.
NGFW/EDR/ITDR telemetry all ingested into XSIAM (panw_ngfw_traffic_raw, xdr_data, ad_audit / okta_sso / msft_azure_ad_signin) so the correlation engine can join on the source host.

payload.code:
# Three coordinated signals from one src_host within the ±30s correlation window.
# NDR leg — outbound HTTP beacon to a known IOC domain.
for i in 1 2 3 4 5; do
  curl -sSL -m 10 -A 'Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; CortexSimMP005/1.0)' "http://testmynids.org/uid/index.html?signal=ndr&iter=$i" -o /dev/null || true
  sleep 2
done
# EDR leg — interactive bash under the www-data service identity.
bash -c 'id; whoami; uname -a; curl --version' 2>&1 | head -20
# ITDR leg — Kerberos pre-auth failure burst against seeded service accounts.
ITDR_REALM=${CORTEXSIM_ITDR_REALM:-CORP.LOCAL}
for svc in svc-sql svc-backup svc-webapp svc-jenkins svc-monitor; do
  echo "Wrong-Password!" | timeout 5 kinit "${svc}@${ITDR_REALM}" 2>&1 | head -2 || true
  sleep 1
done

expected_artifacts.description: Repeated outbound HTTP sessions from the src_host to a known IOC domain.
Interactive bash spawned under the www-data service identity on the src_host.
Kerberos pre-auth (4768) failures against multiple seeded service accounts from one client.

cleanup.code:
rm -f /tmp/cortexsim_mp005_*
echo '[CortexSim] SIM-MP-005 cleanup complete - no persistent artifacts'

biocs.name: MP-005 Interactive Bash From www-data Service Context
biocs.description: XDR BIOC — interactive bash from www-data service context (process lineage anchor). The endpoint leg of the cross-plane stitch: an interactive shell under a non-login web service identity anchors the process lineage that the correlation rule joins to the network and identity signals.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("bash", "sh")
| filter actor_effective_username in ("www-data", "nobody", "apache", "nginx")
| comp count() as proc_count by agent_hostname, action_local_ip, actor_effective_username
| filter proc_count >= 1
| fields agent_hostname, action_local_ip, actor_effective_username, proc_count

biocs.name: MP-005 NGFW Outbound HTTP Beacon To Known IOC Domain
biocs.description: NDR BIOC — outbound HTTP beacon from the src_host to a known IOC domain (testmynids.org). The network leg of the cross-plane stitch, joined to the endpoint lineage by source host/IP.
biocs.logic:
dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| filter app in ("web-browsing", "ssl", "http") or dst_port in (80, 443)
| filter dst_host = "testmynids.org"
| comp count() as beacon_hits by src_ip, dst_host
| filter beacon_hits >= 5
| fields src_ip, dst_host, beacon_hits

biocs.name: MP-005 Identity Kerberos PreAuth Failure Burst From One Client
biocs.description: ITDR BIOC — Kerberos pre-auth (4768) failure burst against multiple seeded service accounts from one client_ip, with an IdP sign-in fallback across okta_sso / msft_azure_ad_signin. The identity leg of the cross-plane stitch, joined by source host/IP.
biocs.logic:
dataset = ad_audit
| filter event_id = 4768
| filter result_code != "0x0"
| comp count_distinct(target_user_name) as targeted_users, count() as failures by client_ip
| filter targeted_users >= 3
| fields client_ip, targeted_users, failures

xql_queries.name: MP-005 NGFW Outbound HTTP To Known IOC testmynids
xql_queries.query:
dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter dst_host = "testmynids.org"
| filter app in ("web-browsing", "ssl", "http") or dst_port in (80, 443)
| comp count() as hits by src_ip, dst_host
| filter hits >= 5
| fields src_ip, dst_host, hits

xql_queries.name: MP-005 EDR Interactive Bash From Service Context
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("bash", "sh")
| filter actor_effective_username in ("www-data", "nobody", "apache", "nginx")
| fields _time, agent_hostname, action_local_ip, actor_effective_username, action_process_image_command_line

xql_queries.name: MP-005 ITDR Kerberos PreAuth Failure Burst Against Service Accounts
xql_queries.query:
dataset = ad_audit
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_id = 4768
| filter result_code != "0x0"
| comp count_distinct(target_user_name) as targeted_users, count() as failures by client_ip
| filter targeted_users >= 3
| fields client_ip, targeted_users, failures

xql_queries.name: MP-005 Cross-Plane Stitch Single Incident Spanning Multiple Products
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| comp count_distinct(_product) as plane_count, count_distinct(incident_id) as incident_count by action_local_ip
| filter plane_count >= 2 and incident_count = 1
| fields action_local_ip, plane_count, incident_count

correlation_rules.name: Cross-Plane Stitch — EDR Plus NDR Plus ITDR From One Source Host
correlation_rules.logic: (BIOC(MP-005 Interactive Bash From www-data Service Context) AND (BIOC(MP-005 NGFW Outbound HTTP Beacon To Known IOC Domain) OR BIOC(MP-005 Identity Kerberos PreAuth Failure Burst From One Client))) WITHIN 30s GROUP BY src_host HAVING count_distinct(_product) >= 2

analytics_modules: Cortex XSIAM Correlation Engine — Cross-Plane Causality (EDR + NDR + ITDR)
Cortex XSIAM Analytics — Multi-Source Incident Stitching by Source Host

products.evidence_query: preset = xdr_data | filter actor_process_image_name in ("bash","sh") | filter actor_effective_username = "www-data" | fields _time, agent_hostname, action_local_ip
dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw | filter dst_host = "testmynids.org" | comp count() as hits by src_ip
preset = xdr_data | comp count_distinct(_product) as plane_count, count_distinct(incident_id) as incident_count by action_local_ip | filter plane_count >= 2 and incident_count = 1

use_cases.name: Cross-Plane Correlation MOAT — EDR + NDR + ITDR Stitch
use_cases.description: Validate that three coordinated signals from one src_host stitch into a single XSIAM incident whose causality chain spans the endpoint, network and identity planes.

test_cases.objective: Each per-plane anchor detection fires from its respective telemetry source.
preconditions: EDR, NDR and ITDR modules all reporting into the same XSIAM tenant from the same src_host.
BIOCs from this card imported and enabled.
steps: Run SIM-MP-005 steps 1-3 (NDR beacon, EDR www-data shell, ITDR Kerberos burst).
Confirm each per-plane validation XQL returns >= 1 row.
success_criteria: XQL `MP-005 NGFW Outbound HTTP To Known IOC testmynids` returns >= 1 row.
XQL `MP-005 EDR Interactive Bash From Service Context` returns >= 1 row.
XQL `MP-005 ITDR Kerberos PreAuth Failure Burst Against Service Accounts` returns >= 1 row.

test_cases.objective: The three planes stitch into a single incident within the ±30s window.
success_criteria: XQL `MP-005 Cross-Plane Stitch Single Incident Spanning Multiple Products` returns >= 1 row with plane_count >= 2 and incident_count = 1.
Correlation `CR-MP-0010` raises one cross-plane incident spanning {EDR, NDR, ITDR} with mttd <= 60s.

preventive_controls: Run application services under least-privilege accounts with no interactive shell.
Apply default-deny egress so beacon callbacks to unknown destinations are blocked at the NGFW.
Enforce strong service-account passwords and disable Kerberos pre-auth-less accounts to blunt spraying/roasting.
detection_engineering: Author per-plane anchor detections (endpoint shell, network beacon, identity pre-auth burst) keyed to a common source identifier.
Build a correlation rule that joins the planes within a tight window and asserts count_distinct(_product) >= 2 under one incident.
Track the Cross-Source Correlation Rate over a run batch — flaky stitching is a real-world failure mode worth catching.

references.title: Unit42 - Cross-Plane Attack Chains in Enterprise Breaches
